document.title: Getting Started with Python and Jupyter Notebook
document.author: Atakelty Hailu

sections.title: Introduction
sections.content: This unit uses Python, a general-purpose programming language widely adopted in science, engineering, and data analysis. Python combines readability with powerful libraries for numerical computation (numpy), data manipulation (pandas), symbolic mathematics (sympy), and visualisation (matplotlib).

subsections.title: Two Ways to Run Jupyter Notebook

options.name: Miniconda (Local, Recommended)
options.description: A lightweight Python installer (~200 MB) that includes Python, conda, and pip. Install only the packages you need.
cons: Requires installing packages separately (easy via conda)
options.best_for: Regular use on your own laptop (recommended)

options.name: Anaconda (Local, Full)
options.description: The full Python distribution (~3 GB) with hundreds of pre-installed packages. Most students won't need the extras.
options.best_for: Users who prefer a GUI launcher or want all packages pre-installed

options.name: Google Colab (Cloud)
options.description: A free, browser-based Jupyter environment hosted by Google.
pros: Works on any device with a browser
options.best_for: Quick access, Chromebooks, or when you cannot install software

subsections.recommendation: We recommend Miniconda — it is lightweight, fast to install, and includes everything you need. Use Google Colab as a backup. Full Anaconda also works but is much larger.

subsections.title: Installing Miniconda (Recommended)
steps: Download the installer for your operating system (Windows, macOS, or Linux)
Run the installer, accepting the default options
Open a terminal (or Anaconda Prompt on Windows) and run: conda install jupyter numpy matplotlib pandas
Launch Jupyter Notebook by typing: jupyter notebook
subsections.note: Already have Anaconda? That works too — it already includes Jupyter and all the packages above. With either Miniconda or Anaconda, you get conda — a package manager that makes installing additional packages easy (e.g. conda install sympy).

subsections.title: Using Google Colab
steps: Sign in with your Google account
Click New Notebook or upload a .ipynb file
Colab comes with numpy, matplotlib, pandas, and sympy pre-installed

subsections.title: The Learning Curve
subsections.advice: Start early, build knowledge gradually. The investment pays off beyond this course. Students who feel lost in Week 0 become confident users by mid-semester.

sections.title: The Jupyter Notebook Environment
sections.content: Jupyter Notebooks contain cells of two main types.

cell_types.purpose: Write text, equations (LaTeX), and insert images

subsections.title: Comments
subsections.syntax: # precedes a comment; everything after # on a line is ignored

subsections.title: Functions Use Parentheses
subsections.explanation: Python functions work like f(x) in algebra — arguments go inside parentheses.

subsections.title: Case Sensitivity
subsections.note: Python is case-sensitive: print works, Print does not. Jupyter highlights recognised commands.

subsections.title: Operators

categories.example: x += 1 is shorthand for x = x + 1

subsections.precedence: Follows BODMAS/PEMDAS. Use parentheses when in doubt.

subsections.title: Indentation Defines Code Blocks
subsections.note: Unlike R (which uses {}), Python uses indentation for if, for, while, and function definitions.

key_functions.name: np.linspace(start, stop, n)
key_functions.description: n equally spaced values from start to stop

vectorised_example.note: The formula applies to all elements simultaneously.

sections.title: Defining Your Own Functions

example.code:
def fahrenheit_to_celsius(F):
    C = (F - 32) * (5/9)
    return C

fahrenheit_to_celsius(68)

sections.note: Starting from Week 0, you will define Python functions to represent scientific models.

sections.title: Working with Data — A Quick Preview

subsections.title: Reading Data from Files (Pandas)
subsections.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.read_csv("data.csv")
df.head()
df.describe()

sections.title: Common Mistakes and How to Avoid Them

mistakes.correct: Add import numpy as np at the top

resources.title: W3Schools Python Tutorial
resources.description: Comprehensive, beginner-friendly tutorial. Cover variables, data types, operators, lists, and functions.

resources.title: NumPy for Beginners
resources.description: Official getting-started guide covering arrays, indexing, and basic operations.

resources.title: Matplotlib Tutorials
resources.description: Official tutorials. Start with 'Pyplot tutorial' for the plotting style used in this unit.

resources.title: SymPy Introduction
resources.description: Symbolic mathematics (derivatives, integrals, solving equations) from Week 4 onward.

resources.title: Google Colab Introduction
resources.description: Interactive introduction to using Colab notebooks.

resources.title: Miniconda / Anaconda Documentation
resources.description: Miniconda download and setup (recommended). For full Anaconda: docs.anaconda.com

resources.title: Real Python
resources.description: High-quality tutorials on a wide range of Python topics.

resources.title: YouTube Tutorials
resources.description: Search for 'Python for beginners Jupyter Notebook'. Corey Schafer's tutorials are widely recommended.

resources.title: Stack Overflow
resources.description: Search for error messages. Someone has almost certainly had the same problem.
